title: Answering Scheduling Questions
learningGoal: The learner will verbally answer questions regarding the time, their age, and the day of the week, correctly utilizing the phonetic exceptions taught in this block.
instructions: In this roleplay, a classmate will ask you three questions. First, they will ask for the time. Then, they will ask the day of the week. Finally, they will ask your age. Respond verbally in Japanese using the information provided in the prompt.

contentItems.text: Remember to use 'desu' at the end of every answer to keep it polite.

speakingPrompt: Answer the audio prompts based on this information: 1) It is 4 o'clock. 2) Today is Monday. 3) You are 20 years old.
scenario: You are sitting in a classroom. A classmate turns to you to confirm some scheduling details.

subTasks.prompt: The classmate asks: 'Ima, nanji desu ka?'. Tell them it is 4 o'clock.
The classmate asks: 'Kyou wa nanyoubi desu ka?'. Tell them today is Monday.
The classmate asks: 'Nansai desu ka?'. Tell them you are 20 years old.

scenarioContext.targetText: すみません。いま、なんじですか。
scenarioContext.baseTranslation: Excuse me. What time is it now?

acceptableParaphrases: The learner can add 'Ima wa' or 'Kyou wa' or 'Watashi wa' to the beginning of their answers, but it is not strictly required.

criteria.name: Exception Usage
criteria.description: Did the learner correctly say 'yo-ji' and 'hatachi' instead of 'yon-ji' and 'ni-juu-sai'?

criteria.name: Sentence Completion
criteria.description: Did every answer end properly with 'desu'?

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: よじです。げつようびです。はたちです。

feedbackRules.onPass: Perfect! You confidently used the numerical exceptions in a conversation.
feedbackRules.onFail: Make sure you do not say 'yon-ji'. It is 'yo-ji'. And remember that 20 years old is 'hatachi'.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort! Practice your days of the week so you can recall 'getsuyoubi' faster.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: すみません。いま、なんじですか。よじです。きょうは なんようびですか。げつようびです。なんさいですか。はたちです。

hintText: Focus strictly on the three requested items: 4, Monday, and 20.
